- Found the email's diagnosis accurate — duplicate DMARC records are indeed ignored by receiving mail servers, creating spoofing vulnerability.
- Verified the fix advice was correct: deleting only the onsecureserver.net record (GoDaddy leftover) while keeping the `p=none` record was appropriate for the use case (avoids prematurely quarantining legitimate service emails like Jobber/CallRail).
- Confirmed the fix had not been applied yet despite Roy's claim ("he did fix this") — deletion likely incomplete or unsaved at GoDaddy.
- SPF record validated as correct; no action needed on that front.
- Advised Spencer to log into GoDaddy DNS settings and manually delete the onsecureserver.net DMARC record, then report back for re-verification.
